dialogueChunks.plotStep: The transmutation held. Common iron, transformed into something that gleams like starlight and weighs half as much. Three years, my savings, my health, my friendships: all for this moment. {target_npc} was the first person I showed it to. They stared at it for a full minute without speaking, then quietly said they were sorry for doubting me. The question now is whether what I gained was worth what {quest_threat} took from me. I don't have an answer yet. I may never have one.
dialogueChunks.outro: You believed in me when no one else did. This piece is yours: the first successful transmutation in history. Whatever happens to me from here, that exists because of both of us.

playerResponses.text: It's beautiful. Now go find your family and show them too.
Three years well spent. Just don't forget the people you left behind.
Worth it? I suppose time will tell. Congratulations.

dialogueChunks.intro: I didn't sleep last night. Not because I was working: because I was sitting by the forge, thinking.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: {quest_focus} is real. The blade holds. Indefinitely. I tested it against stone, against iron, against everything I could find. Not a scratch, not a nick. {target_npc} spread the word, and three settlements have already sent messengers asking about the process. But my house is someone else's now. My family is somewhere I can't follow. I'm standing in an empty workshop holding the most important weapon ever forged, and I have no one to celebrate with except you.
dialogueChunks.outro: Take this. The first unbreakable blade. I'll make more for the settlements. Maybe if I arm enough people, protect enough lives, the cost starts to make sense. Maybe.

playerResponses.text: The blade is remarkable. But rebuilding your life matters too.
You changed the world. Now let the world help you change your life back.

dialogueChunks.intro: It worked, {player_name}. Trial four hundred and twenty-two. It worked.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: The compound purified a patch of corrupted soil in under an hour. Grass is already growing where nothing has lived for years. {target_npc} brought the whole settlement to watch, and there wasn't a dry eye among them. The land heals. After everything: the failed experiments, the lost partnerships, {quest_threat}. The land actually heals. I collapsed afterward. The healer says I've been running on stubbornness and not much else for months.
dialogueChunks.outro: I have enough compound for one more demonstration. After that, I'll need to rest: really rest, for the first time in years. Take this as payment, and know that every field that grows green again owes something to you.

playerResponses.text: Green grass on dead land. That was worth every trip into the wilds.
Rest. Eat. You've proven your point. Now prove you can survive it.
